name: Bay State Housing Stability Fund
provides: Operating grants of $25k-$100k for organizations delivering eviction prevention and rapid rehousing services.
eligibility_notes: Must have a Massachusetts 501(c)(3) and at least two years of direct housing service delivery.

name: Commonwealth Emergency Shelter Grant Program
provides: State reimbursement funding for emergency shelter beds and supportive services.
eligibility_notes: Requires existing shelter licensing and capacity to manage cost-reimbursement contracts.

name: Greater Boston Food Access Collaborative
provides: Network membership giving access to shared cold storage, bulk purchasing, and a regional food bank pipeline.
eligibility_notes: Open to any food pantry or meal program operating within the Greater Boston region.

name: Harvest Share In-Kind Produce Program
provides: Weekly donated fresh produce and refrigerated transport for meal and pantry programs.
eligibility_notes: Requires capacity to receive and distribute perishable goods within 48 hours.

name: Mystic River Youth Opportunity Grant
provides: Seed grants of $10k-$30k for out-of-school-time and youth mentoring programs.
eligibility_notes: Targets early-stage organizations with annual budgets under $250k serving youth ages 10-18.

name: Pathways Workforce Development Fund
provides: Program grants of $50k-$150k for job training, credentialing, and placement initiatives.
eligibility_notes: Must demonstrate employer partnerships and track job placement outcomes.

name: New England Immigrant Welcome Initiative
provides: Grants of $20k-$75k for legal aid, ESOL, and immigrant integration services.
eligibility_notes: Programs must primarily serve immigrants, refugees, or asylum seekers.

name: Liberty Legal Aid Pro Bono Network
provides: Volunteer attorney referrals for clients needing immigration or protective-order representation.
eligibility_notes: Partner organizations must screen and refer eligible clients; no direct funding provided.

name: Open Hands Fiscal Sponsorship Collective
provides: Comprehensive fiscal sponsorship including 501(c)(3) status, grant management, and back-office accounting.
eligibility_notes: Best for projects without their own tax-exempt status and budgets under $250k.

name: Charles River Fiscal Sponsor Program
provides: Fiscal sponsorship and grant administration for emerging arts, environmental, and education projects.
eligibility_notes: Accepts projects aligned with arts, environment, or education in the Greater Boston area.

name: Foundation for Nonprofit Capacity
provides: Cohort-based coaching in board development, fundraising, and financial management.
eligibility_notes: Designed for early-stage Massachusetts nonprofits with budgets under $500k.

name: TechBridge Nonprofit IT Donation Program
provides: Discounted and donated laptops, software licenses, and managed IT support.
eligibility_notes: Requires verified 501(c)(3) status; some products limited to budgets under $1M.

name: Beacon Mental Health Access Grant
provides: Grants of $40k-$120k to expand community-based behavioral health and counseling services.
eligibility_notes: Clinical services must be delivered by licensed providers.

name: SAMHSA Community Behavioral Health Block Grant
provides: Federal pass-through funding for substance use and mental health treatment programs.
eligibility_notes: Requires state agency partnership and significant compliance reporting capacity.

name: Second Chance Reentry Support Fund
provides: Grants of $30k-$90k for programs supporting formerly incarcerated individuals.
eligibility_notes: Programs must include employment, housing, or wraparound reentry services.

name: Safe Harbor Domestic Violence Services Grant
provides: Grants of $35k-$100k for survivor advocacy, emergency housing, and safety planning.
eligibility_notes: Must maintain confidential service protocols and trained advocacy staff.

name: Veterans Forward Reintegration Grant
provides: Grants of $25k-$80k for veteran employment, housing, and peer support programs.
eligibility_notes: Programs must primarily serve U.S. military veterans.

name: Golden Years Senior Services Partnership
provides: Referral network for home-delivered meals, transportation, and aging-in-place support.
eligibility_notes: Open to organizations serving adults aged 60 and older.

name: Able Access Disability Inclusion Grant
provides: Grants of $15k-$50k to improve accessibility and disability inclusion in programming.
eligibility_notes: Prioritizes organizations led by or primarily serving people with disabilities.

name: GreenRoots Environmental Justice Fund
provides: Grants of $10k-$40k for community-led environmental and climate resilience projects.
eligibility_notes: Focuses on environmental justice work in low-income and immigrant neighborhoods.

name: ArtsForward Creative Community Grant
provides: Project grants of $5k-$25k for community arts, cultural programming, and youth arts education.
eligibility_notes: Open to arts organizations and programs with a public community benefit.

name: Commonwealth Volunteer Match Network
provides: Statewide platform matching skilled and general volunteers to nonprofit needs.
eligibility_notes: Free to any registered Massachusetts nonprofit.

name: Boston Cares Skilled Volunteer Corps
provides: Recurring teams of skilled volunteers for tutoring, mentoring, and program delivery.
eligibility_notes: Requires a designated volunteer coordinator on staff.

name: First Light Early-Stage Catalyst Grant
provides: Unrestricted catalyst grants of $5k-$20k for newly formed nonprofits.
eligibility_notes: For organizations less than three years old with budgets under $150k.

name: Cornerstone Capacity Building Institute
provides: Executive coaching, strategic planning facilitation, and data systems consulting.
eligibility_notes: Sliding-scale fees; designed for organizations with budgets of $250k-$2M.

name: Federal CDBG Public Services Allocation
provides: Municipal pass-through funding for public services benefiting low- and moderate-income residents.
eligibility_notes: Requires city/town subrecipient agreement and HUD compliance reporting.

name: New England Reentry Housing Coalition
provides: Coalition membership with shared housing placement resources and policy advocacy.
eligibility_notes: Open to organizations serving people transitioning from incarceration.

name: Nourish New England Food Equipment Grant
provides: Donated commercial kitchen equipment, refrigeration units, and delivery vehicles.
eligibility_notes: Requires a facility capable of housing donated equipment.

name: Horizon Family Foundation General Operating Grant
provides: Flexible general operating support grants of $50k-$250k across all mission areas.
eligibility_notes: Invitation and full application required; highly competitive national pool.

name: Cambridge Community Resilience Microgrant
provides: Fast-turnaround microgrants of $2k-$15k for grassroots community resilience efforts.
eligibility_notes: Limited to grassroots groups based in or primarily serving the Cambridge/Somerville area.
